news.headline: EpilepsyGTx Partners with ClearPoint Neuro to Support Targeted Delivery of Lead Gene Therapy in Epilepsy
news.summary: EpilepsyGTx will use ClearPoint's SmartFlow Cannula and Navigation System to deliver its gene therapy candidate EPY201 in an upcoming first-in-human Phase 1/2a trial for focal refractory epilepsy, expected to start in the UK/Europe by end of 2026.

news.headline: ClearPoint Neuro (NASDAQ:CLPT) Shares Down 7.5% – What's Next?
news.summary: CLPT shares fell 7.5% in early July as investors weighed persistent margin pressure and continued cash burn against the company's revenue-growth story, a pullback following the stock's roughly doubling after the mid-June uniQure/FDA news.

news.headline: ClearPoint Neuro CEO Burnett Sells $387,600 in Company Shares
news.summary: CEO Joseph Burnett sold 20,000 shares at a weighted average price of $19.38 under a pre-established Rule 10b5-1 trading plan, following exercise of options at $2.50 per share.

news.headline: B. Riley raises ClearPoint Neuro price target to $30 from $20, maintains Buy
news.summary: B. Riley analyst Anderson Schock raised his price target on ClearPoint Neuro to $30 from $20 while keeping a Buy rating, citing the improved regulatory outlook for partner uniQure's AMT-130 gene therapy, which is delivered using ClearPoint's navigation platform.

news.headline: ClearPoint Neuro Stock Surges on Partner uniQure's Favorable FDA Path for Huntington's Gene Therapy
news.summary: CLPT shares jumped roughly 26-32% after partner uniQure said the FDA agreed that 3-year Phase I/II data for AMT-130 (delivered via ClearPoint's navigation platform and SmartFlow cannula) can support a BLA for accelerated approval, with submission planned for Q3 2026.

news.headline: Stifel reiterates ClearPoint Neuro stock rating on neurosurgery platform
news.summary: Following the biennial American Society for Stereotactic and Functional Neurosurgery meeting, Stifel reiterated a Buy rating and $16 price target on ClearPoint Neuro, highlighting the company's combined platform spanning drug delivery, brain-computer interfaces and its newly announced focused-ultrasound capability.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings release
catalysts.detail: ClearPoint Neuro is expected to report second-quarter 2026 financial results, with the market watching for progress against the $52-56M full-year revenue guidance; exact date not yet formally announced.

catalysts.label: uniQure BLA submission for AMT-130 (Huntington's disease)
catalysts.detail: Partner uniQure plans to file a Biologics License Application with the FDA for AMT-130, delivered via ClearPoint's navigation platform and SmartFlow cannula, using 3-year Phase I/II data following supportive Type B FDA meeting feedback -- the single biggest near-term revenue catalyst for ClearPoint's biologics/drug-delivery business.

catalysts.label: EpilepsyGTx first-in-human trial start (EPY201)
catalysts.detail: EpilepsyGTx plans to begin a first-in-human Phase 1/2a trial of EPY201 for focal refractory epilepsy in the UK/Europe using ClearPoint's SmartFlow Cannula and Navigation System, enrolling up to 30 adults.

catalysts.label: IRRAS integration synergies / reduced cash burn
catalysts.detail: Company guidance calls for cost synergies from the IRRAS acquisition to begin reducing cash burn in H2 2026, supporting the path to the $52-56 million full-year revenue target.

catalysts.label: CE Mark decisions for PRISM Laser Therapy systems
catalysts.detail: Both the 1.5T and 3.0T PRISM Laser Therapy systems have been submitted for CE Mark approval in Europe, expected in the second half of 2026.

pulse: ClearPoint Neuro's momentum builds around its partners' pipelines: uniQure's supportive June FDA feedback on AMT-130 (delivered via ClearPoint's platform) drove a sharp CLPT rally toward a 52-week high near $30, and a new EpilepsyGTx delivery partnership announced July 14 broadens the drug-delivery ecosystem further. Underlying fundamentals are improving too, with Q1 2026 revenue up 43% to $12.1 million and full-year guidance of $52-56 million reaffirmed as IRRAS integration progresses, though shares have since pulled back to around $16-19 on lingering margin and cash-burn concerns even as B. Riley (PT $30) and Stifel (PT $16) both maintain Buy ratings. The stock remains high-beta and partner-dependent, with the next major checkpoints being ClearPoint's own Q2 2026 earnings (expected mid-August) and further clarity on uniQure's BLA timeline.
